campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending an emergency city rule and moving forward with plans for a bicycle bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including submitting grant applications to help pay for it.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 to youth scholarship fund
Extension of emergency ordinance #2326
Bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant applications for Hamilton Avenue overcross project

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council met to decide how to distribute grant money to community organizations and events. They approved funding recipients and amounts for both a competitive grant program and community events, and made updates to the rules for how groups can apply for money to host events or close streets.
keyTopics: Approved specific organizations receiving grant funding and how much money each will get
Decided on the budget for the Competitive Event Grant Program for the current fiscal year
Updated the rules and application process for Community Event and Street Closure Grants

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on April 1, 2026 focused on providing residents access to the meeting through multiple channels with live translations available in over 50 languages. The council made arrangements for people to watch and participate remotely via cable, online streaming, phone, and Zoom.
keyTopics: Live translation services available in 50+ languages
Multiple ways to watch: Cable Channel 26, YouTube, Zoom, or phone
No public comment period was available for this meeting
Online access through Wordly translation platform and San José's official channels

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: On March 30, 2026, the Mountain View City Council held a meeting to approve previous meeting minutes and hear public comments from residents. The council also discussed upcoming meetings for various city boards and committees, with details about where and when those meetings will be held.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comments and questions from residents
Upcoming board and committee meeting schedules
Recreation Division contact information for agenda questions

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community development projects in the city. The group approved notes from their previous February meeting and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to support local housing and human services programs.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding applications for affordable housing projects
Community development block grants (CDBG) being distributed to local programs
Approval of meeting minutes from February 2026

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on helping local businesses grow and succeed through several new programs. They discussed offering amnesty to businesses that haven't had proper licenses, reaching out to shopping centers, and creating campaigns to encourage residents to shop locally. The city is also exploring partnerships with private companies to sponsor events and offer incentives to help retail stores and startups thrive.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program to help unlicensed businesses get compliant
Outreach campaigns to shopping centers and retail businesses
Summer shop local marketing campaign
Public-private partnerships to fund city events and sponsorships
Economic incentives for small businesses and startups

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's Historical and Landmarks Commission is holding a hearing on April 2, 2026, where residents can attend in person or join online via Zoom. The meeting will discuss matters related to the city's historic buildings and landmark designations, and residents can submit written comments by noon on the day of the meeting.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom)
Deadline for written public comments by noon on April 2
Remote participation via Zoom (ID: 972 3326 2035)

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to handle several routine items, including a proclamation celebrating Women's History Month. The council approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and authorized a three-year contract with Insight Public Sector for email security software services costing about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Emergency facilities agreement with American Red Cross
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late agenda items added to meeting packet
